---
title: "DNS name resolution not working"
date: 2018-09-15
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by lewisforlife on 2018-09-15
I can ping external ip addresses, but not host names. Here is what /etc/resolv.conf looks like:

```

# Generated by NetworkManager
search home
nameserver 127.0.1.1

```

If I edit it to look like this then name resolution starts working (ie I can ping google.com, ubuntuforums.org etc):

```

# Generated by NetworkManager
search home
nameserver 127.0.1.1
nameserver 8.8.8.8

```


If I reboot my computer my /etc/resolv.conf reverts to the original without the nameserver 8.8.8.8 and dns no longer works again. Any help would be much appreciated.

---

### Post by lewisforlife on 2018-09-15
Oh yeah, btw I am using Ubuntu 17.10

---

### Post by jeremy31 on 2018-09-15
Well 17.10 has not been supported since 22 July.  Time to change to a supported version

---

### Post by lewisforlife on 2018-09-15
Based on your response I just upgraded to 18.04, lol.

Funny enough DNS is now working, but there is still a ? (question mark) over the networking icon in the top right like something is wrong. Any idea how I can tell what is causing that?
